title: Service Outcomes, Boundaries, and Server Lifecycle
description: A civic incident API has endpoints but no stated user outcome, ownership boundary, startup contract, dependency readiness, shutdown policy, or evidence connecting a request to durable effect. Learners cumulatively produce service outcome, boundary, and lifecycle map through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define a server outcome with user, client, operator, security, and accessibility evidence instead of treating endpoint count as success.
Trace user agent, client, proxy, gateway, origin handler, dependency, and response roles without assigning one component evidence it cannot observe.
Distinguish process, listener, connection, stream, request, handler, goroutine, dependency, and durable-state lifetimes.
